text.title: TLS Validation CI Gate Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the cicd-tls-validation-gate methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: The gate MUST require SSL Labs / testssl.sh grade A+; A or lower MUST fail the pipeline.

rationale.source: SSL Labs grading docs, Mozilla TLS guidelines

rule.rationale: Anything below A+ indicates weak ciphers, deprecated protocol versions, or HSTS gaps; the gap costs cents to fix in CI and percent-revenue at outage.

rule.statement: Cipher suite MUST match Mozilla 'intermediate' or 'modern' configuration; weak ciphers (RC4, 3DES, CBC with TLS 1.0) MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Mozilla's intermediate profile is the practical floor for compatibility with modern clients.

rule.statement: Strict-Transport-Security header MUST be served with `max-age >= 31536000` (1 year) and `includeSubDomains`.
rule.rationale: Short HSTS max-age leaves first-visit downgrade attacks; the 1-year floor is the OWASP recommendation.

rule.statement: OCSP stapling MUST be enabled; bare OCSP queries leak browsing patterns and add latency.
rule.rationale: Without stapling each TLS handshake queries the CA; staple cuts handshake latency and improves privacy.

rule.statement: Pipeline promotion to production MUST be gated on a successful TLS scan; an unconditional 'continue-on-error' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: A gate that doesn't block isn't a gate; the whole point is to prevent regression.

rule.statement: If the service has no public TLS endpoint (internal-only, behind VPN), this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Public-grade TLS scans against private endpoints either fail to reach or grade against irrelevant criteria.
